step_by_step_reasoning: First, we identify the external source of PDF files labeled 'PDF Images', mapping it to a ThirdParty node. This triggers 'AWS Lambda Data Automation' which uploads files to 'Amazon S3'. An S3 upload event triggers 'AWS Lambda Scan and Process'. This Lambda orchestrates calls to 'Amazon Textract' for OCR and 'Amazon Comprehend' for medical classification, saving results to 'Amazon DynamoDB'. For manual review of exception cases, a team of agents ('UserCompanyAgent') accesses a React application hosted on 'Amazon ECS' which communicates via 'Amazon API Gateway' to fetch data from DynamoDB. A legacy 'Application' node is present on the board to represent the destination system, though no direct arrows are drawn to it.

graph.name: DXC Claim Automation Solution
graph.notes: Serverless claims processing system that uses AI/ML to automate text extraction and medical classification, reducing manual processing time from 4.5 minutes to under 1.5 minutes per claim.

nodes.name: PDF Images
nodes.notes: Source of medical claim images and PDFs.

nodes.name: AWS Lambda Data Automation
nodes.notes: Automates the ingestion and upload of PDF documents.

nodes.name: AWS Lambda Scan and Process
nodes.notes: Processes images using AI services and writes results to DynamoDB.

nodes.name: Amazon Textract
nodes.notes: Extracts text from scanned claims images.

nodes.name: Amazon DynamoDB
nodes.notes: Stores metadata and extracted medical codes.

nodes.name: Amazon API Gateway
nodes.notes: Exposes APIs for the React review application.

nodes.name: Amazon ECS React App
nodes.notes: Hosts the stateless React web frontend.

nodes.name: Exception Cases Team
nodes.notes: Operators who review and correct low-confidence claims.

nodes.name: Legacy Application
nodes.notes: The target system where processed claims are entered.

edges.notes: PDF images are sent for ingestion.
Lambda uploads PDFs to S3 bucket.
S3 triggers the processing Lambda function.
Lambda sends image to Textract and receives extracted text.
Lambda queries Comprehend to classify medical codes.
Lambda writes extracted data to DynamoDB.
API Gateway retrieves claim data from DynamoDB.
React frontend is served to the review team.
Reviewers make API requests to load and review claim data.
